prose:
Method provenance block
Property: Condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for nitrobenzene; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ (highest susceptibility toward nucleophilic attack).
Level of theory: Density-functional theory, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (PySCF backend).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz); single-point population analyses for neutral (N), cationic (N−1), and anionic (N+1) states were performed with this fixed geometry (DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model applied).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1) for the parent; cationic and anionic states treated as doublets.
Software: PySCF (via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill). Optimizer not applicable; SCF cycles for each charge state converged.
Convergence & warnings: SCF convergence achieved for all three charge states.  The integrity checker returned three **warnings** (verbatim):
• "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "common header present"
These do not indicate failure (integrity.status = ok; trustworthy = true) but are reproduced here in full per reporting standards.

Headline result
Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺): atom 4 (C, index 3 in 0-based numbering) with f⁺ = 0.4964178 (dimensionless).
Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻): atom 5 (C, index 4) with f⁻ = 0.1072882.
